title: poa idahoensis
intro: Poa idahoensis, also known as Idaho fescue, is a perennial bunchgrass native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Poa idahoensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Poa idahoensis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
